text: This article explores how the recent shift in practical theology towards the area of study known as “lived religion” provides a hermeneutic for understanding the ways in which the Harry Potter novels are potentially influencing the spiritual lives and self-identifications of teenagers. Given that many more people today than ever before are searching for spiritual meaning and experience in contexts other than institutional religious settings, lived theology can serve as a key for unlocking and making sense of the ways in which everyday life has become increasingly structured by non-religious, yet still spiritually significant, cultural objects such as novels like Harry Potter.
headline: Fully Human Written
